categories.name: Intercity & High Speed
categories.title: SpotRail HQ  ·  Intercity & High Speed
categories.subtitle: 125 mph+ long-distance express services · Pendolino, Azuma, Eurostar, Voyager, Aurora, Evero, IC225
rows: Legacy fleet; mostly replaced by e320. Reduced to ~5 sets in service.
Main Eurostar fleet; 17 sets in service.
West Coast Main Line — Euston to Glasgow, Manchester, Liverpool, Birmingham
56 sets; tilting technology allows higher speeds on curves.
Dual-voltage (25kV AC on HS1 / 750V DC third rail)
High Speed 1 (HS1) St Pancras–Kent; also conventional 750V DC routes
140 mph on HS1 only; 100 mph on conventional lines. 29 six-car sets.
East Coast Main Line (LNER); Great Western Main Line (GWR)
IEP programme. LNER fleet branded Azuma; GWR fleet branded IET.
Electric-only variant of IEP; used on fully electrified ECML sections.
Great Western Railway; TransPennine Express; Hull Trains
GWML (GWR — branded Castle); WCML Anglo-Scottish (TPE — branded Nova 1); ECML open access (Hull Trains — branded Paragon)
Same IEP platform as 800/801. TPE Nova 1 replaced Class 185s on Anglo-Scottish services.
East Coast Main Line (fully electrified sections)
5 sets; smaller electric-only order for ECML.
13 sets in service from Jun 2024. ORR issued improvement notice regarding continuous speed supervision; retrofit in progress.
10 sets; in full passenger service from November 2024. Replacing Class 221 on Avanti services.
Midland Main Line — London St Pancras to Nottingham, Derby, Sheffield
33 sets ordered. First entered service 3 Dec 2025; 2 deliveries/month through 2026. Replacing Class 222.
East Coast Main Line — London King's Cross to Edinburgh/Leeds
Design speed 140 mph; operated at max 125 mph in service. 12 locos remain; ~4 daily diagrams as of 2026. Being displaced by Azuma fleet.
Cross-country long-distance — Birmingham to Glasgow, Edinburgh, NE England, SW England, South Coast
34 four-car sets. Diesel throughout; no electric pickup. CrossCountry sole operator.
CrossCountry; Avanti West Coast(retiring); Grand Central
Cross-country long-distance (CrossCountry); ECML open access Bradford–London (Grand Central: 2 sets)
CrossCountry: ~36 sets. Grand Central: 2 sets. Avanti units transferred to CrossCountry in 2024 as Class 805/807 arrived.
Midland Main Line (EMR); ECML open access London–Stirling (Lumo: 5 sets); ScotRail (22 sets from 2026)
Being displaced on EMR by Class 810 Aurora. Units cascading to Lumo and ScotRail.
WCML Anglo-Scottish — Liverpool Lime Street / Manchester Airport to Glasgow / Edinburgh
12 five-car sets. Electric counterpart to Class 802 (Nova 1 bi-mode) on TPE Anglo-Scottish services.

categories.name: Regional Passenger
categories.title: SpotRail HQ  ·  Regional Passenger
categories.subtitle: Inter-regional & cross-country services · Sprinters, Turbostars, Civity, Coradia, Adelante, Flirt, Voyager XC
rows: Transport for Wales (12 units); DB Cargo UK (11, some stored); Colas Rail (2)
TfW Premier Service loco-hauled (Cardiff–Holyhead/Manchester); DB Cargo infrastructure; Colas freight
Also used for Chiltern Railways push-pull and LNER rescue DVT duties. 26 locos total.
Transport for Wales; Direct Rail Services; Chiltern Railways
TfW loco-hauled services; DRS nuclear flask/departmental haulage; Chiltern Mainline push-pull with Mk5A coaches
34 locos. DRS primary fleet owner; subleased for passenger duties. New locos 68019-68032 delivered to Chiltern Jan 2026.
Transport for Wales; ScotRail; Northern; South Western Railway; East Midlands Railway; Great Western Railway
Trans-Wales; Scottish intercity; Northern regional; Waterloo–Exeter (SWR)
~370 vehicles across multiple operators. Largest single-class fleet of DMUs in UK.
Waterloo to Exeter / Salisbury / Yeovil Junction
30 vehicles. Similar to Class 158 but purpose-built for SWR routes.
Thames Valley (Reading/Oxford/Bedwyn — GWR); Chiltern Main Line (Chiltern)
CrossCountry; East Midlands Railway; Northern; ScotRail; West Midlands Railway
Cross-country regional (CrossCountry); East Midlands regional (EMR); Northern regional; ScotRail inter-city
~139 vehicles. Two and three-car variants across operators.
20 vehicles (two-car and three-car sets). Operated by Southern, part of Govia Thameslink Railway group.
West Midlands regional routes (Birmingham–Dorridge, Birmingham–Kidderminster, Stourbridge Junction branch)
~39 vehicles. Entire UK fleet now with West Midlands Trains.
Wales & West routes — re-leased to GWR from Transport for Wales end of 2023
27 vehicles. Withdrawn from TfW end 2023; returned to service with GWR planned Dec 2025.
ECML open access Bradford/Sunderland–London (Grand Central); Hull–London (Hull Trains)
~35 vehicles. Originally built for First Great Western; now fully in open-access sector.
Trans-Pennine routes — Manchester/Liverpool to Leeds, Newcastle, Edinburgh
51 three-car sets. Entire fleet with TPE; working alongside Class 802 (Nova 1) on main routes.
Northern regional routes (Leeds, Manchester, Liverpool, Yorkshire, NW England corridors)
58 vehicles (25 two-car + 33 three-car). Full fleet with Northern.
Birmingham–Shrewsbury; Birmingham–Hereford; Leamington–Nuneaton; Camp Hill Line
26 vehicles (12 two-car + 14 four-car). Operated by West Midlands Railway sub-brand.
Great Western Railway; Gatwick Express (Southern); Great Northern (GTR)
Heathrow Express service (GWR); Gatwick Express London Bridge/Victoria–Gatwick (Southern); Great Northern commuter (GTR)
~160 vehicles across operators. Replaced Class 332 on Heathrow services.
Liverpool Street–Norwich intercity (745/0: 10 units); Stansted Express Liverpool Street–Stansted Airport (745/1: 10 units)
20 units (10 × 745/0 + 10 × 745/1). 745/0 replaced Class 90 loco-hauled Norwich sets; 745/1 replaced Class 379.
East Anglia rural / branch lines; Norwich area services
38 units (14 × 755/3 three-car + 24 × 755/4 four-car). Replaced Classes 153, 156 and 170 on East Anglia non-electrified routes.
Northern; Transport for Wales; Great Western Railway
Mixed electrified/non-electrified regional routes across Northern, Wales and GW areas
~28 vehicles. Converted from Class 319 dual-voltage EMUs for bi-mode operation.

categories.name: Commuter & Suburban
categories.title: SpotRail HQ  ·  Commuter & Suburban
categories.subtitle: Stopping and metro-style mainline services · all suburban EMU/DMU classes including Elizabeth line, Overground, Merseyrail
rows: Great Western Railway; Northern; Transport for Wales
Short-distance stopping services across GW, Northern and Wales regions
~120 vehicles. Two-car and three-car variants; still working many rural/suburban routes.
Northern; Great Western Railway; Transport for Wales; East Midlands Railway
Rural branch lines and lightly-used stopping services
~65 vehicles. Single-car units — used where demand is very low.
~9 vehicles. Two-car units; originally built as Class 155 but most converted to Class 153s.
Transport for Wales; West Midlands Trains
~7 vehicles. Converted from redundant London Underground D78 Stock.
Formerly Moorgate–Welwyn Garden City / Hertford North; Great Northern area
Withdrawn from all passenger service. Last operated on Great Northern/Southern routes. Some units preserved.
Fully withdrawn 9 December 2022. Replaced by Class 720 (Aventra). One unit (315856) preserved.
North London / Hertford / Welwyn routes (Overground); Liverpool Street outer suburban (Greater Anglia)
~90 vehicles. Dual-voltage capable units used by London Overground on AC-only routes.
Manchester/Blackpool area stopping (Northern); West Midlands commuter (WMT)
~65 vehicles. Originally built for Thameslink; cascaded north as Class 700 replaced them.
Glasgow suburban — North Clyde Line, Argyle Line, Cathcart Circle area
~22 vehicles (three-car units). Class 320/4 sub-variant capable of 100 mph.
Liverpool Street outer suburban (Greater Anglia); some units converted/transferred to ScotRail
~110 vehicles. Some units sold/transferred; mainly Greater Anglia remaining fleet.
34 vehicles with Northern (9 stored). West Midlands Trains units fully withdrawn September 2024 — replaced by Class 730.
Northern commuter — Leeds, Manchester, Liverpool corridors; Calder Valley; York–Leeds
~50 vehicles (31 three-car + 12 four-car sets). Modern replacement for older Northern EMUs.
All 14 units withdrawn from service 2020 and scrapped by 2021. Replaced by Class 387 (GWR/Heathrow Express service).
Leeds suburban — Wharfedale Line, Airedale Line
~16 vehicles (four-car sets). Capable of 100 mph but operated at 90 mph in service.
Glasgow suburban — North Clyde Line, Helensburgh–Edinburgh, Airdrie–Balloch
Elizabeth line — Reading/Heathrow to Paddington to Liverpool Street to Abbey Wood / Shenfield
WCML commuter — London Euston to Birmingham, Crewe, Liverpool (London Northwestern); Birmingham suburban (WMR)
~50 active vehicles with WMT (Class 350/2s being returned as Class 730 arrives). Some sub-classes dual-voltage.
London, Tilbury and Southend line — Fenchurch Street to Southend Central / Shoeburyness
74 vehicles. c2c is sole operator. Maximum line speed 75 mph despite 100 mph capability.
East Midlands Railway; Global Centre of Rail Excellence (GCRE)
East Midlands Railway commuter services; 3 units at GCRE (Wales) for R&D / testing
~21 active EMR vehicles. Originally Heathrow Connect / FCC; transferred to EMR as Heathrow Connect service ended.
Formerly King's Cross commuter — London to Peterborough / Cambridge / Horsham
Withdrawn; acquired by Eversholt Rail 2021. All but 3 vehicles scrapped. Replaced by Class 387 and Class 717.
Kent commuter and regional — Charing Cross / Cannon Street / Victoria to Kent
36 five-car sets. DC-only; metro-style high-frequency operation.
South London, Brighton Main Line, Thameslink (Southern / GTR routes)
~200+ vehicles. Largest Electrostar fleet. Multiple sub-variants across routes.
London Overground orbital routes — East London Line, North London Line, West London Line
Great Northern services — London King's Cross area; reactivated 2025 after transfer from Greater Anglia
30 units. Withdrawn from Greater Anglia 2022; purchased by Porterbrook 2024 and re-leased to Great Northern. ~12 active with more phasing in.
Glasgow–Ayr, Glasgow–Inverclyde / Wemyss Bay, Edinburgh suburban
38 vehicles (22 three-car + 16 four-car). All with ScotRail.
Edinburgh suburban; ECML Scotland commuter; Glasgow–Edinburgh via Shotts
Formerly South Western Railway — Waterloo to Bournemouth / Weymouth fast services
Withdrawn March 2021. All 18 units scrapped. Replaced by Class 444 and Class 701.
Waterloo fast and stopping services — Surrey and Hampshire commuter routes
Waterloo inner suburban — South West London, Surrey stopping services
~85 four-car units. Older fleet; being supplemented by Class 701.
Formerly South Western Railway — Waterloo suburban
Fully withdrawn January 2022. All units withdrawn from SWR service.
Waterloo suburban — Ascot, Reading, Alton, Guildford routes
~90 four-car and five-car units. Refurbished by Alstom — £25m programme completed December 2024; speed increased to 100 mph.
Kent metro — inner suburban London to Kent stopping services
~146 four-car units. Part of the Networker family with Class 466.
Kent metro — inner suburban services (can only work in multiple with Class 465 since 2021)
Isle of Wight — Ryde Pier Head to Shanklin
5 two-car units. Design speed 60 mph; operated at max 45 mph on Island Line.
Thameslink core route — Bedford / Luton to City of London to Gatwick / Brighton; also some Southern routes
Waterloo suburban and fast routes; replacing older Class 455/456 fleet
~60 units. Aventra platform; phased introduction from January 2024.
Kent commuter routes — inner and outer suburban services
30 five-car units. Transferred from South Western Railway to Southeastern during 2023–2024.
London Overground — Lea Valley, Gospel Oak–Barking, Watford DC, Romford–Upminster lines
Great Northern — Moorgate to Welwyn Garden City / Hertford North (includes deep-tube-compatible Moorgate section)
Liverpool Street inter-urban and commuter — Ipswich, Norwich, Southend (Greater Anglia); Fenchurch Street–Southend (c2c)
~89 five-car units (Greater Anglia) + additional c2c units. Replaced Classes 315 and 321 at Greater Anglia.
West Midlands Trains (West Midlands Railway + London Northwestern Railway)
Birmingham Cross-City Line (3-car 730/0 at 90 mph); WCML commuter Euston–Birmingham/Crewe (5-car 730/2 at 110 mph)
48 × three-car (730/0) + 36 × five-car (730/2). First entered service Nov 2023; replacing Classes 319 and 323 in WMT fleet.
Electric (750V DC third rail) + battery (777/1 sub-class)
Merseyrail all lines — City Line, Northern Line, Wirral Line (Liverpool Central hub)
53 units total: 46 × standard 777/0 + 7 × battery-electric 777/1. Replaced Class 507/508; Class 508 withdrawn Jan 2024, Class 507 withdrawn Nov 2024.
Fully withdrawn from Merseyrail service 28 November 2024. Replaced by Class 777. One unit (507001) preserved.
Fully withdrawn from Merseyrail service January 2024. Replaced by Class 777.

categories.name: Freight
categories.title: SpotRail HQ  ·  Freight
categories.subtitle: Freight haulage and yard shunting · Classes 08, 56, 60, 66, 69, 70, 73, 86, 88, 90, 92
rows: DB Cargo; Freightliner; Network Rail; Various operators and industry
Yard and depot shunting throughout UK; industrial sidings
~150 active. 996 total built — largest single-class locomotive in UK history. Many preserved on heritage railways.
Yard shunting — higher maximum speed variant of Class 08 for faster shunting moves
~8 active. 26 built. Higher gearing than Class 08 gives faster maximum speed.
Colas Rail Freight; GB Railfreight; UK Rail Leasing
Heavy freight and infrastructure / test train haulage
135 built. Small operational fleet remains. Colas Rail primary current operator; GBRf acquired 16 units. Many stored.
GB Railfreight; DCRail Freight; Colas Rail Freight
Heavy bulk freight — steel, oil, aggregate, coal traffic
100 built. ~15 mainline active as of 2025. Colas sold their fleet; now with GBRf, DCRail and DB Cargo.
DB Cargo (~165); GB Railfreight (~115); Freightliner; Direct Rail Services; Colas Rail; DRS
General freight throughout UK — intermodal containers, bulk commodities, automotive, infrastructure
~430 operational. Most numerous mainline diesel loco in UK. Effectively the standard UK freight locomotive.
General freight and GB Railfreight operations
16 locos (rebuilt Class 56 bodies with EMD powerplants). All with GBRf. Final unit delivered August 2025.
Intermodal container freight and bulk commodities
36 in UK fleet (Freightliner: ~17, Colas: ~17). GE-built powerplants.
Electro-Diesel (750V DC third rail + diesel 600 hp)
Caledonian Sleeper haulage (non-electrified sections north of Edinburgh); Network Rail infrastructure trains
~28 active (Class 73/9 rebuilt sub-variant). GBRf primary operator for sleeper duties. 90 mph in electric mode; slower on diesel.
Withdrawn from all mainline service. 3 preserved examples remain for occasional heritage use. DB Cargo and Greater Anglia both retired their fleets.
Bi-mode (25kV AC electric 5,360 hp + diesel 940 hp)
Direct Rail Services; Special Charter services
Intermodal freight on West Coast Main Line — electric mode under wire; diesel mode off-wire
10 locos; all with DRS. First dedicated bi-mode freight locomotive in UK. Electric mode is primary; diesel for last-mile.
Heavy intermodal freight on WCML and ECML
~30 locos. Freightliner now primary operator after inheriting ex-Greater Anglia and ex-DB Cargo units. DB Cargo retired their 90s in 2023.
Dual-voltage electric (25kV AC overhead / 750V DC third rail)
Channel Tunnel freight services (DB Cargo); Caledonian Sleeper loco haulage on electrified sections (GBRf)
~26 active in UK. 87 mph maximum speed. Also operated by SNCF in France (classified CC 92000) on Channel Tunnel services.

categories.name: Charter & Railtours
categories.title: SpotRail HQ  ·  Charter & Railtours
categories.subtitle: Enthusiast charter trains & railtours · Class 20/37/47/57 diesels + all mainline-certified preserved steam locomotives
rows: Loram UK; Balfour Beatty; Harry Needle Railroad Co; West Coast Railway Co; heritage operators
Departmental / infrastructure trains; enthusiast railtours; heritage railway use
~15 mainline-registered operational as of 2025. Iconic 'nose-to-nose' working pairs. Many preserved on heritage railways.
Colas Rail; Rail Operations Group (ROG); West Coast Railway Co; Network Rail (as Class 97/3 for ERTMS testing)
Railtours and charter passenger; departmental; nuclear flask haulage; infrastructure testing; freight
~60 mainline operational. Beloved by enthusiasts. ROG use for moving EMU/DMU consists. Class 97/3 variant for ERTMS cab signalling testing in Wales.
West Coast Railway Co (WCRC); heritage operators
Enthusiast charter railtours; loco-hauled excursions; heritage railway use
~25 mainline-registered. WCRC largest operator for railtours. Classic railtour engine; many in long-term storage.
Diesel-electric (Ruston 2,750 hp — rebuilt from Class 47 bodyshells)
West Coast Railway Co (57/0); GWR Night Riviera (57/6)
GWR Night Riviera sleeper rescue/standby loco (57/6 sub-class: 57602–57605); charter railtours (57/0); occasional freight (57/3)
33 locos total (12 × 57/0; 16 × 57/3; 5 × 57/6). 57/6 sub-class dedicated Night Riviera locos at Long Rock depot.
New-build loco; only new main-line steam locomotive built in UK in modern times. Regularly hauls The Cathedrals Express and other railtours.
Mainline railtours UK-wide; occasional static display at NRM York
World's most famous locomotive. First to officially reach 100 mph. NRM-owned; mainline certified.
Named after LNER Chief Mechanical Engineer. Holds steam speed record of 112.5 mph (set in preservation 1959).
Union of South Africa — LNER A4
Privately owned A4 Pacific; regular railtour performer.
Mainline railtours; NRM York on display when not on tour
NRM-owned; one of the most powerful express steam locos built for British railways.
Privately owned; regular main-line performer on WCML and other routes.
Princess Margaret Rose — LMS Princess Royal
Mainline railtours; based at Midland Railway Centre Butterley
Mainline certified; occasional railtour performer alongside Duchess of Sutherland.
Mainline railtours; Keighley & Worth Valley Railway base
Operated by WCRC; one of three mainline-active Jubilees.
Regularly works 'The Cathedrals Express' and Pullman excursions.
British India Line — SR Merchant Navy
Recently returned to mainline service after overhaul.
WCRC-operated; regular on The Jacobite and other tours.
City of Wells — SR Battle of Britain
Frequently works Golden Arrow and other Pullman-style excursions.
NRM-owned; last steam loco to haul a BR passenger train in 1968.
Mainline railtours; based at Mid-Hants Railway
One of two mainline-certified 9Fs (also 92214). Most powerful preserved British steam loco.
Mainline railtours (WCRC operations); The Jacobite service
One of several mainline-certified Black Fives. WCRC uses multiple certified examples.

categories.name: Light Rail & Metro
categories.title: SpotRail HQ  ·  Light Rail & Metro
categories.subtitle: Urban rapid transit — London Underground, DLR, Overground, Elizabeth line, city trams and regional metros
rows: Central line & Waterloo and City line stock
London Underground — Central line + Waterloo & City line
London Underground uses a unique four-rail system (outer +420V, inner −210V = 630V total). Being replaced by new Deep Tube trains in future.
London Underground — Northern line + Jubilee line
1995 Stock: Northern line. 1996 Stock: supplementary Jubilee line order. Both use same design.
Fully automatic train operation (ATO); drivers supervise but trains drive themselves. One of the most automated metros in the world.
London Underground — Metropolitan, District, Hammersmith & City, Circle lines
Largest LU fleet. Air-conditioned. Subsurface lines being progressively upgraded to 750V DC. S7 = 7-car; S8 = 8-car (Metropolitan line).
Being replaced by 2024 Stock (Siemens Inspiro). Oldest operational LU fleet. Withdrawal begins H2 2026 as new trains enter service.
New Piccadilly line trains (Siemens Inspiro London)
94 trains ordered at £1.5bn. First entry into passenger service expected H2 2026 (subject to delay). Will increase peak frequency from 24 to 27 trains/hour by 2027.
DLR — B90 / B92 / B2K / B07 / B09 Stock
Driverless automated operation. Multiple build batches from Bombardier and BN. Being progressively replaced by new CAF B23 fleet.
54 trains at ~£350m. First three entered service Sep 2025 but withdrawn Nov 2025 due to wet-weather braking issue; software fix in progress. Full entry expected 2026.
East London Line, North London Line, West London Line. Dual-voltage for AC and DC sections.
London Overground (TfL) — Lea Valley, Gospel Oak–Barking, Watford DC, Romford–Upminster
DC-only routes. Part of the Aventra family.
Elizabeth line (TfL) — Reading/Heathrow through central London to Abbey Wood/Shenfield
Opened through central section May 2022 (Crossrail). Capacity ~1,500 passengers per train.
Tyne & Wear Metro — Newcastle, Sunderland, Gateshead, Airport
UK's only 1,500V DC overhead system. Original fleet being progressively replaced by new Stadler CAF units.
First units entered service December 2024; final delivery February 2026. Full fleet replacing original Metrocars.
Entered service December 2023; fully replaced previous fleet by June 2024. Driverless-capable (currently driver-operated). World's third oldest underground railway.
Merseyrail — City Line, Northern Line, Wirral Line
Full fleet in service; Class 507 and 508 both fully withdrawn. 777/1 battery units can operate on non-electrified extensions.
UK's largest tram network; 100+ km of route. Multiple depot stabling points across Greater Manchester.
Edinburgh Trams — Airport to York Place / Newhaven
27 trams including additional units for 2023 Newhaven extension. 100% low-floor; longest trams in UK at 42.8 m.
West Midlands Metro (WMCA) — Birmingham city centre and beyond
Urbos 100 units are newer and have battery for catenary-free operation in city centre. Extensions to Edgbaston, Wolverhampton and further ongoing.
Original fleet still in use. 10 new Bombardier Flexity Swift trams on order. Yellow, Blue, Purple and Tram-Train routes.
Mixed fleet: 15 × Bombardier Incentro (Line 1, from 2004) + 22 × Alstom Citadis 302 (Lines 2 & 3, from 2015).
Blackpool Tramway — Starr Gate to Fleetwood

UK's only first-generation tramway in continuous use. Modern Flexity 2 fleet (16 original + 2 additional 2018) alongside heritage Balloon cars (heritage service suspended Dec 2024; expected to return 2025).

categories.name: Heritage & Preserved
categories.title: SpotRail HQ  ·  Heritage & Preserved
categories.subtitle: Heritage-only steam & preserved classic diesel — run on preserved railways ONLY · See Charter tab for mainline-certified steam
rows: 3 preserved (6000 King George V, 6023 King Edward II, 6024 King Edward I)
Great Western Society / Didcot Railway Centre; Tyseley Locomotive Works
Three examples; 6000 static at Swindon STEAM Museum; 6023/6024 at Didcot.
Severn Valley Railway; GWSR; Didcot; various
Largest surviving class of GWR express steam locos.
Severn Valley Railway; Didcot Railway Centre; various
Highly successful mixed-traffic design; widespread preservation.
Improved version of original Hall; final development of the class.
Lighter version of the Hall for secondary routes; two mainline certified.
Most numerous GWR locomotive class (863 built). Ubiquitous pannier tank design.
South Devon Railway; Dartmouth Steam Railway; various
Small prairie tank for GWR branch and suburban workings.
NRM-owned; on display at York. Considered too large/heavy for mainline certification currently.
2 overseas (60008 Dwight D. Eisenhower in USA; 60010 Dominion of Canada in Canada)
National Railroad Museum, USA; Canadian Railway Museum
Both returned to UK temporarily for 75th anniversary (2013). Normally on static display overseas.
~14 on heritage railways (in addition to mainline-certified examples)
East Lancs Railway; KWVR; Severn Valley Railway; Strathspey Railway; various
18 total preserved; ~4 are mainline certified (listed in Charter tab). Remainder work heritage railways.
~6 on heritage railways (in addition to mainline-certified)
Severn Valley Railway; East Lancashire Railway; Midland Railway Centre; various
8 total preserved in UK. 2 mainline certified (in Charter tab); remainder on heritage railways.
Lakeside & Haverthwaite Railway; Keighley & Worth Valley Railway; Caledonian Railway; various
Development of Stanier 2-6-4T; good passenger tank design.
Bluebell Railway; NRM; Bodmin & Wenford Railway
Most powerful 4-4-0 locomotive design in UK. Three examples preserved.
Only surviving U Class; based on the Mid-Hants Railway.
Swanage Railway; Bluebell Railway; East Lancashire Railway; Spa Valley Railway; various
~21 total preserved; 3 mainline certified (in Charter tab). Remainder work heritage railways.
Namesake of the class; under restoration. Oliver Cromwell (70013) is the mainline-certified example (Charter tab).
Mid-Hants Railway; Severn Valley Railway; KWVR; various
~6 total preserved; 2 mainline certified (Charter tab). Remainder on heritage railways.
Bluebell Railway; Severn Valley Railway; KWVR; various
Versatile tank engine; five examples survive across the UK.
Mid-Hants Railway; Great Central Railway; various
Tender version of the Standard 4; five examples survive.
Great Central Railway; East Lancashire Railway; Severn Valley Railway; various
9 total preserved; 2 mainline certified (Charter tab). Most powerful standard BR steam design.
Industrial heritage sites; colliery museums; various small heritage railways
Most numerous preserved steam locomotive type in UK. Built for wartime and industrial use.
Keighley & Worth Valley Railway; Churnet Valley Railway
Wartime design for heavy freight; simple and robust.
East Lancashire Railway; NRM; various heritage sites
Popular heritage railway diesel; 20 examples survive.
Strathspey Railway; Bo'ness & Kinneil Railway; various
Associated with Scottish Highland lines; several examples preserved in Scotland.
East Lancashire Railway; Barrow Hill Roundhouse; NYMR
Iconic 1950s/60s express diesel; named after their distinctive turbocharger whistle.
Midland Railway Centre; East Lancashire Railway; Peak Rail; various
Named after mountain peaks; associated with Midland Main Line services.
Severn Valley Railway; West Somerset Railway; various
18 examples preserved — unusually high survival rate. Popular with enthusiasts; associated with GW Main Line.
Severn Valley Railway; Didcot Railway Centre; West Somerset Railway
Unique diesel-hydraulic transmission; associated with Western Region. 7 of 74 built survive.
NRM; Barrow Hill; NYMR; Locomotion; various
No (3 occasionally work specials at Barrow Hill)
Napier Deltic twin-engine design; 22 built, 6 survive. Synonymous with ECML express workings in the 1960s–80s.
